items.primary_command: add tests for untested production modules — prioritize by import count

items.summary: The resolver API hides operational capabilities behind concrete types.

evidence: `types.Resolver` exposes only `Resolve`, but `cmd/picodns/main.go` also needs `SetObsEnabled`, cache stats, NS cache stats, delegation stats, and transport addr stats.
`buildResolver` therefore returns the interface plus three concrete resolver pointers so startup and shutdown code can keep reaching around the abstraction.
The concrete-only methods are operationally important, not rare test hooks, so the API surface is incomplete for real runtime use.
detail.suggestion: Define an explicit runtime-facing resolver surface instead of returning parallel concrete handles. For example: add a small `types.RuntimeResolver`/`types.ResolverStats` capability interface set for observability and snapshots, implement it in `Cached`, `Recursive`, and `Upstream`, then change `buildResolver` to return one composed value. Validate with `go test ./...` after removing concrete resolver plumbing from `cmd/picodns/main.go`.

items.primary_command: desloppify plan done "review::.::holistic::api_surface_coherence::resolver_interface_leaks_concrete_ops::f8ab2b21" --note "<what you did>" --attest "I have actually [DESCRIBE THE CONCRETE CHANGE YOU MADE] and I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing."

items.summary: The UDP transport implementation silently ignores the documented caller timeout contract.

evidence: `types.Transport.Query` documents that any `timeout > 0` is used as the exchange timeout.
`udpTransport.Query` caps caller-provided timeouts to `t.timeout`, so a larger timeout request is silently shortened.
`Recursive.queryServersParallel` computes adaptive per-hop timeouts before calling the transport, so the caller is explicitly depending on that contract.
detail.suggestion: Either honor the interface exactly by using the supplied timeout as-is when `timeout > 0`, or change the interface comment and all callers to document that transports may cap timeouts. The safer path is to preserve caller intent in `udpTransport.Query`, then add regression tests for shorter, equal, and longer caller-provided timeouts with `go test ./internal/resolver`.

items.primary_command: desloppify plan done "review::.::holistic::contract_coherence::transport_timeout_contract_violation::6c8bbea0" --note "<what you did>" --attest "I have actually [DESCRIBE THE CONCRETE CHANGE YOU MADE] and I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing."

items.summary: Resolver constructors mutate a process-wide tracer registry as a hidden side effect.

evidence: `internal/obs/tracer.go` exposes a mutable process-global `GlobalRegistry` used for all perf reporting.
Each resolver-related constructor registers tracers immediately on creation, so allocating a resolver changes global process state before the caller opts into reporting.
`internal/server/utils.go` later emits a single report from the same global registry, which means tracer ownership is process-wide rather than tied to one server/resolver instance.
detail.suggestion: Introduce an explicit observability runtime object and inject it into resolver/server construction instead of registering into `obs.GlobalRegistry` from constructors. Stage it as: 1. define a registry interface in `internal/obs`; 2. thread it through `NewRecursive`, `NewCached`, `NewTransport`, `newConnPool`, and `newRTTTracker`; 3. let `cmd/picodns/main.go` create one registry only when perf reporting is requested; 4. update report generation to use that instance; 5. validate with `go test ./...` and a perf-build test that constructing two resolvers does not merge tracer state unless requested.

items.primary_command: desloppify plan done "review::.::holistic::initialization_coupling::constructor_side_effect_registry::891bd670" --note "<what you did>" --attest "I have actually [DESCRIBE THE CONCRETE CHANGE YOU MADE] and I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing."

items.summary: TCP request handling builds a connection-scoped context but does not pass it across the resolver seam.

evidence: `internal/server/tcp.go` creates `connCtx` and a goroutine that closes the socket when that context ends, signaling an intent to scope work to the connection lifetime.
The actual resolver call still uses `s.resolver.Resolve(ctx, buf)` with the outer server context, so in-flight resolution is not canceled when the TCP client disconnects.
`internal/server/tcp_test.go` covers normal request/response flow but has no test for client disconnect during a slow resolution, which is why the seam mismatch is currently invisible.
detail.suggestion: Pass `connCtx` into `Resolve`, and add a TCP test with a blocking mock resolver that asserts cancellation when the client closes early. Run `go test ./internal/server` after the change to lock in the lifecycle behavior.

items.primary_command: desloppify plan done "review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance::tcp_connection_context_not_propagated::4e803d5e" --note "<what you did>" --attest "I have actually [DESCRIBE THE CONCRETE CHANGE YOU MADE] and I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing."

items.summary: Tests miss the runtime seams that are currently drifting: timeout honoring, connection cancellation, and config isolation.

evidence: There is no test asserting that a caller-provided `Transport.Query` timeout is honored by `udpTransport.Query`, even though the interface documents that behavior.
`internal/server/tcp_test.go` does not simulate client disconnect while resolution is blocked, so the unused `connCtx` path is not exercised.
`internal/config/config_test.go` does not protect against repeated binding/parsing in one process, despite `config.go` using package-global bindings and the default flag set.
detail.suggestion: Add seam-focused regression tests in stages: 1. `internal/resolver` test for transport timeout behavior against the documented interface; 2. `internal/server` test that closing a TCP client cancels a blocking resolver via `connCtx`; 3. `internal/config` test that parsing with isolated `FlagSet`s works repeatedly in one process. Validate with `go test ./...`.

items.primary_command: desloppify plan done "review::.::holistic::test_strategy::missing_regression_tests_for_runtime_contracts::1a90cfd5" --note "<what you did>" --attest "I have actually [DESCRIBE THE CONCRETE CHANGE YOU MADE] and I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing."
